title: Script P4: Assigning Viral Taxonomy
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f9rfu","text":"This protocol provides a method for identifying bacteriophage/virus taxonomy without a virome dataset using UniProt reference database. Based on the methods found in the following publication:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fo9rt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bvfjg","text":"Hannigan, Geoffrey D., et al. \"The Human Skin Double-Stranded DNA Virome: Topographical and Temporal Diversity, Genetic Enrichment, and Dynamic Associations with the Host Microbiome.\" mBio 6.5 (2015): e01578-15.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

Files","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aoalv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4o3fe","text":"Output:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"t2fl","text":"Bray-curtis_virome_analysis/contig_otu_table_transposed_formatted.txt","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9qcvs","text":"Phage_Taxonomy/order_rel_abund.tsv","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"218rs","text":"Phage_Taxonomy/genus_rel_abund.tsv","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9h7ef","text":"Phage_Taxonomy/species_rel_abund.tsv","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4pl3q","text":" before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"f7045","text":"Perl scripts and other supplementary information available at:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"57s11","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9redm","text":"https://figshare.com/articles/The_Human_Skin_dsDNA_Virome_Topographical_and_Temporal_Diversity_Genetic_Enrichment_and_Dynamic_Associations_with_the_Host_Microbiome/1281248","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":171}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"B4264147304640B88B15FB71A5028217","url":"https://figshare.com/articles/The_Human_Skin_dsDNA_Virome_Topographical_and_Temporal_Diversity_Genetic_Enrichment_and_Dynamic_Associations_with_the_Host_Microbiome/1281248"}}}}
steps: Download the entire Uniprot TrEMBL reference fasta database. mkdir ./references/TrEMBLcd ./references/TrEMBLwget ftp://ftp.uniprot.org/pub/databases/uniprot/current_release/knowledgebase/complete/uniprot_trembl.fasta.gzgunzip ./TrEMBL/uniprot_trembl.fasta.gzmkdir ../UniProt-Virus-Phagecd ../UniProt-Virus-Phage mkdir ./references/TrEMBL cd ./references/TrEMBL wget ftp://ftp.uniprot.org/pub/databases/uniprot/current_release/knowledgebase/complete/uniprot_trembl.fasta.gz gunzip ./TrEMBL/uniprot_trembl.fasta.gz mkdir ../UniProt-Virus-Phage cd ../UniProt-Virus-Phage
Download the virus taxonomy reference text file. wget ftp://ftp.uniprot.org/pub/databases/uniprot/current_release/knowledgebase/taxonomic_divisions/uniprot_trembl_viruses.dat.gz
Unzip the files. gunzip ./uniprot_trembl_viruses.dat.gz

To make the TrEMBL database more managable downstream, we want to pull out the viral reference genes to make a virus specific database. While still in the directory 'UniProt-Virus-Phage', get a list of the IDs associated with each virus protein sequence.

Before getting the sequences that match the accession number list, we need to remove the block format from the fasta files using the perl script remove_block_fasta_format.pl https://figshare.com/articles/The_Human_Skin_dsDNA_Virome_Topographical_and_Temporal_Diversity_Genetic_Enrichment_and_Dynamic_Associations_with_the_Host_Microbiome/1281248 Perl scripts can be found in the supplementary information and on figshare . cd ../TrEMBL/perl remove_block_fasta_format.pl ./uniprot_trembl.fasta ./uniprot_trembl_no_block.facd ../UniProt-Virus-Phage cd ../TrEMBL/ perl remove_block_fasta_format.pl ./uniprot_trembl.fasta ./uniprot_trembl_no_block.fa cd ../UniProt-Virus-Phage

Use the perl script filter_fasta_file.pl to get all of the sequences with matching accession numbers. https://figshare.com/articles/The_Human_Skin_dsDNA_Virome_Topographical_and_Temporal_Diversity_Genetic_Enrichment_and_Dynamic_Associations_with_the_Host_Microbiome/1281248 Perl scripts can be found in the supplementary information and on figshare . perl filter_fasta_file.pl -l ./virus_accession_trembl_list.txt -i ./references/TrEMBL/uniprot_trembl_no_block.fa -o ./uniprot_phage_virus_TrEMBL.fa -id_regex

Generate blast database from the uniprot references (virus+phage). makeblastdb -dbtype prot -in uniprot_phage_virus_TrEMBL.fa -out uniprot_virus_and_phage_TrEMBL_dbegrep ./tmp1_TrEMBL.txt sed 's/^.*\(Viruses\)/\1/' ./tmp1_TrEMBL.txt | sed 's/ \+/ /g' | sed 's/\.//g' | sed 's/; /\t/g' | sed 's/ /_/g' > ./tmp_taxa_tree_TrEMBL.txt" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="E23EF1195AB141F0BDBA8D19377CC080" > makeblastdb -dbtype prot -in uniprot_phage_virus_TrEMBL.fa -out uniprot_virus_and_phage_TrEMBL_db egrep "^(OC|ID|OS)" ./uniprot_trembl_viruses.dat | sed -e :a -e '$!N;s/\n\(OS\)/ /;ta' -e 'P;D' | sed -e :a -e '$!N;s/\n\(OC\)/ /;ta' -e 'P;D' > ./tmp1_TrEMBL.txt sed 's/^.*\(Viruses\)/\1/' ./tmp1_TrEMBL.txt | sed 's/ \+/ /g' | sed 's/\.//g' | sed 's/; /\t/g' | sed 's/ /_/g' > ./tmp_taxa_tree_TrEMBL.txt

Pull out the different taxonomic level information. https://figshare.com/articles/The_Human_Skin_dsDNA_Virome_Topographical_and_Temporal_Diversity_Genetic_Enrichment_and_Dynamic_Associations_with_the_Host_Microbiome/1281248 Perl scripts can be found in the supplementary information and on figshare . perl get_format_order.pl tmp_taxa_tree_TrEMBL.txt tmp_order_TrEMBL.txtperl get_format_family.pl tmp_taxa_tree_TrEMBL.txt tmp_family_TrEMBL.txtperl get_format_subfamily.pl tmp_taxa_tree_TrEMBL.txt tmp_subfamily_TrEMBL.txtperl get_format_genus.pl tmp_taxa_tree_TrEMBL.txt tmp_genus_TrEMBL.txt perl get_format_order.pl tmp_taxa_tree_TrEMBL.txt tmp_order_TrEMBL.txt perl get_format_family.pl tmp_taxa_tree_TrEMBL.txt tmp_family_TrEMBL.txt perl get_format_subfamily.pl tmp_taxa_tree_TrEMBL.txt tmp_subfamily_TrEMBL.txt perl get_format_genus.pl tmp_taxa_tree_TrEMBL.txt tmp_genus_TrEMBL.txt
Get list of IDs.
Get list of "species names"
Paste together the lists.
Remove the tmp files after the script is finished running. rm ./tmp* WARNING: This will remove all files that start with

Now that we have the virus reference database prepared and formatted, we can start annotating the viruses in our dataset. Instead of annotating each individual short sequence, we are going to annotate our longer contigs. Note that near the end of the loop below, we need to address some name issues. There are duplicates in the TrEMBL dataset due to slight naming differences (i.e. Environmental_Halophage and Environmental_halophage) which could throw off some downstream relative abundance analyses. Therefore we standardize these names across the dataset. We also removed some 'strain' specific information to allow the phages/viruses to be a little more broadly grouped (i.e. Taking the numbers off the end of different Staphylococcus phage names to make them all 'Staphylococcus phage'.
In the main working directory make a directory for the uniprot taxonomy results. mkdir ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs

Perform blastx of the predicted ORFs against the virus/phage uniprot database reference (TREMBL). BLAST Toolkit 2.2.0 Unix ftp://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/blast/executables/blast+/LATEST/ NCBI blastx -query ./glimmer3/output/Contigs_no_block_with_names_glimmer_output_final.fa -out ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/blastx_trembl_glimmer_total_orfs.txt -db ./references/UniProt-Virus-Phage/uniprot_virus_and_phage_TrEMBL_db -outfmt 6 -num_threads 16 -max_target_seqs 1 -evalue 1e-5 Use ORF fasta file (from glimmer3)

Move blastx output files to a specific directory. mkdir ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/blastx_raw_resultsmv ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/blastx_trembl_glimmer_total_orfs.txt ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/blastx_raw_results mkdir ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/blastx_raw_results mv ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/blastx_trembl_glimmer_total_orfs.txt ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/blastx_raw_results

Get the gene and contig IDs from the blastx results (output 6; tab delimited file). Do this all in a loop to make life easier. mkdir ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/virome_phage_blastx_formatted_for_taxafor file in $(ls ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/blastx_raw_results); do mkdir ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/virome_phage_blastx_formatted_for_taxa for file in $(ls ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/blastx_raw_results); do
Set file name variable. NAME=$(echo ${file})
Assign contig IDs to tmp list file.
Assign gene hit IDs to another tmp list.
Paste these files together for reference.
Add in the taxonomy data from the uniprot reference database. echo Assigning taxonomy to ${file}...if [[ $NAME =~ .*trembl.* ]]; thenawk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2
Add @ symbol delimiter at first tab for easier perl parsing.
Filter out the contigs that did not have at least one orf match per 10kb. Return the number of orfs that had assigned taxonomy to each contig.
Assemble a list of contig numbers, only including those contigs which had at least 1 orf per 10kb. awk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $2 in a { print $2
Get only the contig ID numbers, clean away the other information from each row.
Remove the underscores from the ends of the contig IDs so that grep can match the entire words.
Use grep to get the contig rows that match the list of contigs to keep.
Again add @ symbol delimiter at first tab for easier perl parsing on filtered contigs.

Remove the strain specific IDs at the ends of the phage and virus species names (for example, staph_phage_0594 is just staph_phage). https://figshare.com/articles/The_Human_Skin_dsDNA_Virome_Topographical_and_Temporal_Diversity_Genetic_Enrichment_and_Dynamic_Associations_with_the_Host_Microbiome/1281248 Perl scripts and supplementary information available at figshare . Here I am primarily changing the phage identifiers and am not changing much of the other taxonomic information. There were some duplicate that we noticed upon manual inspection, so I also fix those here.

Use the uniprot orf contig taxonomy script for contig taxonomy assignment. perl contig_id_by_orfs.pl ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/virome_phage_blastx_formatted_for_taxa/trimmed_format_for_perl_filtered_${file} ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/virome_phage_blastx_formatted_for_taxa/perl_taxonomy_results_${file}

Add underscore to the end of each contig number in the file. sed 's/\t/_\t/' ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/virome_phage_blastx_formatted_for_taxa/perl_taxonomy_results_${file} > ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/virome_phage_blastx_formatted_for_taxa/perl_taxonomy_results_underscore_${file}done sed 's/\t/_\t/' ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/virome_phage_blastx_formatted_for_taxa/perl_taxonomy_results_${file} > ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/virome_phage_blastx_formatted_for_taxa/perl_taxonomy_results_underscore_${file} done

The contigs have now been annotated with taxonomic information, but this is only half of the analysis battle. Our goal is to get a relative abundance table with each column being a sample, each row being a contig (like an "OTU") which has taxonomic information, and the intersections have the relative abundance information (calculated as RPKM).

Run bowtie2 of the negative cleaned samples against the contig reference database. First build bowtie reference of the contigs. mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abundbowtie2-build -f ./ray_contigs_from_total_cat_pairs/Contigs_no_block_with_names.fasta ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_contig_build mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund bowtie2-build -f ./ray_contigs_from_total_cat_pairs/Contigs_no_block_with_names.fasta ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_contig_build

Map the sequences to the contigs. mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hitsrun.bowtie2.against.contigs () { bowtie2 -x ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_contig_build -f ./negative_clean_seqs/$1 -S ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hits/$1 -L 25 -N 1}export -f run.bowtie2.against.contigs mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hits run.bowtie2.against.contigs () { bowtie2 -x ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_contig_build -f ./negative_clean_seqs/$1 -S ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hits/$1 -L 25 -N 1 } export -f run.bowtie2.against.contigs
Run as a subroutine because it can be run much quicker with multiple procs at a time. ls ./negative_clean_seqs/ | xargs -I {} --max-procs=128 bash run.bowtie2.against.contigs {}

Rename the files to be .sam files. for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hits); do mv ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hits/ for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hits); do mv ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hits/"${file}" ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hits/"${file/%.fa/.sam}" done

Calculate the hit abundances from bowtie2 using bowtie2 estimation perl script. Perl script available at: https://figshare.com/articles/The_Human_Skin_dsDNA_Virome_Topographical_and_Temporal_Diversity_Genetic_Enrichment_and_Dynamic_Associations_with_the_Host_Microbiome/1281248 mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_samfor file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hits); do perl calculate_abundance_from_sam.pl ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hits/${file} ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam/${file}done mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hits); do perl calculate_abundance_from_sam.pl ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/bowtie2_neg_cleaned_hits/${file} ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam/${file} done

Rename the sam files to text files. for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam); do mv ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam/ for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam); do mv ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam/"${file}" ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam/"${file/%.sam/.txt}" done

Add in contig length information using awk and calculate RPKM. mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_with_lengthfor file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam); do export SUM=$(awk '{ SUM += $2 } END { print SUM }' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam/${file}) mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_with_length for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam); do export SUM=$(awk '{ SUM += $2 } END { print SUM }' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam/${file})

Add an echo of the sum value to confirm that the sum is being calculated (read it in STDOUT). echo Sum is $SUM awk --assign sum=$SUM 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { print $1 ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_with_length/${file} done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="5440C461BE8945948CCDD32380F4DB67" > echo Sum is $SUM awk --assign sum=$SUM 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { print $1"\t"$2"\t"a[$1]"\t"$2*1000000000/(a[$1]*sum) }' ./contig_stats/contig_length_without_greater_sign.txt ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_from_sam/${file} > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_with_length/${file} done

Get columns of only the contig IDs and the normalized RPKM abundance counts and add sample name to top of column for when this is used in distance matrix calculations. mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_RPKM_with_only_contig_idfor file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_with_length); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/_R1\.txt//') echo Name is $NAME cut -f 1,4 ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_with_length/${file} | sed ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_RPKM_with_only_contig_id/${file} done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="C46006E231C743988ADD35A9DAE6B2B5" > mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_RPKM_with_only_contig_id for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_with_length); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/_R1\.txt//') echo Name is $NAME cut -f 1,4 ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_with_length/${file} | sed "1 s/^/Contig_ID\t${NAME}\n/" > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_RPKM_with_only_contig_id/${file} done
Make master list of the contig numbers as a reference for merging the relative abundance matrix values.

Merge the sample hit files to the master contig list. After this runs, all of the resulting files should have the same number of lines because they were all merged with the same master list. mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_listfor file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_RPKM_with_only_contig_id); do awk 'FNR==NR {a[$1]=$2;next}{ print $1 ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list/${file} done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="8B96A226F4474FE29FF7C0C57D29C0D7" > mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_RPKM_with_only_contig_id); do awk 'FNR==NR {a[$1]=$2;next}{ print $1"\t"a[$1] }' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_RPKM_with_only_contig_id/${file} ./ray_contigs_from_total_cat_pairs/master_contig_list.txt | sed '/[0-9]\t[0-9]/!s/$/0/' | sed '1 s/\t0//' | sed '1 s/0$//' > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list/${file} done

Get only the RPKM abundance values so that they can be merged to the master contig list and used for distance matrix calculations. mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_RPKM_for_mergefor file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list); do cut -f 2 ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list/$file > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_RPKM_for_merge/${file}done mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_RPKM_for_merge for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list); do cut -f 2 ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list/$file > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/abundance_RPKM_for_merge/${file} done
Merge the abundances with the contig names.
Transpose the cat file. python transpose_tab_delim.py -i ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/contig_otu_table.txt -o ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/contig_otu_table_transposed.txt
Remove underscores from the transposed files as this can interfere with downstream analyses.

This data from the section above (Bray-curtis_virome_analysis/contig_otu_table_transposed_formatted.txt) can be used in Script R3. As stated above, we can now use the relative abundance table to determine the taxonomic composition of our virome samples. This analysis is described below.
The above can be used for taxonomy reference independent diversity, but we will also want to look at virus taxonomy relative abundance.
First make a master contig to ID reference table that includes the ID and the corresponding contig number. awk 'FNR==NR {a[$1]=$2
Make a directory for the taxonomy output. mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_order_rel_abund

Calculate the relative abundance of the viral order. for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/_R1\.txt//') awk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { print $2 ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_order_rel_abund/raw_${file} awk '{a[$1]+=$2}END{for(i in a) print i,a[i]}' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_order_rel_abund/raw_${file} | sed 's/ /\t/g' | sed "s/$/\t${NAME}/" > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_order_rel_abund/rel_abund_${file} done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="99AA922933B04A9B87EFF1358DED1CFF" > for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/_R1\.txt//') awk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { print $2"\t"a[$1] }' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list/${file} ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/contig_id_reference_table.tsv | sed '1d' > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_order_rel_abund/raw_${file} awk '{a[$1]+=$2}END{for(i in a) print i,a[i]}' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_order_rel_abund/raw_${file} | sed 's/ /\t/g' | sed "s/$/\t${NAME}/" > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_order_rel_abund/rel_abund_${file} done

Calculate the relative abundance of the viral families. mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_family_rel_abundfor file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/_R1\.txt//') awk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { print $3 ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_family_rel_abund/raw_${file} awk '{a[$1]+=$2}END{for(i in a) print i,a[i]}' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_family_rel_abund/raw_${file} | sed 's/ /\t/g' | sed "s/$/\t${NAME}/" > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_family_rel_abund/rel_abund_${file} done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="81983453743D457B825703F04292FCA0" > mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_family_rel_abund for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/_R1\.txt//') awk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { print $3"\t"a[$1] }' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list/${file} ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/contig_id_reference_table.tsv | sed '1d' > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_family_rel_abund/raw_${file} awk '{a[$1]+=$2}END{for(i in a) print i,a[i]}' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_family_rel_abund/raw_${file} | sed 's/ /\t/g' | sed "s/$/\t${NAME}/" > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_family_rel_abund/rel_abund_${file} done

Calculate the relative abundance of the viral sub-families. mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_sub_family_rel_abundfor file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/_R1\.txt//') awk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { print $4 ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_sub_family_rel_abund/raw_${file} awk '{a[$1]+=$2}END{for(i in a) print i,a[i]}' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_sub_family_rel_abund/raw_${file} | sed 's/ /\t/g' | sed "s/$/\t${NAME}/" > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_sub_family_rel_abund/rel_abund_${file} done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="ACEAAEB71B1F48DDBFB7F45BA1F7DEB9" > mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_sub_family_rel_abund for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/_R1\.txt//') awk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { print $4"\t"a[$1] }' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list/${file} ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/contig_id_reference_table.tsv | sed '1d' > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_sub_family_rel_abund/raw_${file} awk '{a[$1]+=$2}END{for(i in a) print i,a[i]}' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_sub_family_rel_abund/raw_${file} | sed 's/ /\t/g' | sed "s/$/\t${NAME}/" > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_sub_family_rel_abund/rel_abund_${file} done

Calculate the relative abundance of the viral genera. mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_genus_rel_abundfor file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/_R1\.txt//') awk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { print $5 ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_genus_rel_abund/raw_${file} awk '{a[$1]+=$2}END{for(i in a) print i,a[i]}' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_genus_rel_abund/raw_${file} | sed 's/ /\t/g' | sed "s/$/\t${NAME}/" > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_genus_rel_abund/rel_abund_${file} done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="8EFD6DDAC4804B2186BC01FCCCBAE841" > mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_genus_rel_abund for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/_R1\.txt//') awk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { print $5"\t"a[$1] }' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list/${file} ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/contig_id_reference_table.tsv | sed '1d' > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_genus_rel_abund/raw_${file} awk '{a[$1]+=$2}END{for(i in a) print i,a[i]}' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_genus_rel_abund/raw_${file} | sed 's/ /\t/g' | sed "s/$/\t${NAME}/" > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_genus_rel_abund/rel_abund_${file} done

Calculate the relative abundance of the viral species. mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_species_rel_abundfor file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/_R1\.txt//') awk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { print $6 ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_species_rel_abund/raw_${file} awk '{a[$1]+=$2}END{for(i in a) print i,a[i]}' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_species_rel_abund/raw_${file} | sed 's/ /\t/g' | sed "s/$/\t${NAME}/" > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_species_rel_abund/rel_abund_${file} done" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="A5D486F870364CFF94877962D0AD20F0" > mkdir ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_species_rel_abund for file in $(ls ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list); do NAME=$(echo ${file} | sed 's/_R1\.txt//') awk 'FNR==NR { a[$1]=$2; next } $1 in a { print $6"\t"a[$1] }' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/RPKM_contig_count_on_master_list/${file} ./uniprot_taxonomy_using_orfs/contig_id_reference_table.tsv | sed '1d' > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_species_rel_abund/raw_${file} awk '{a[$1]+=$2}END{for(i in a) print i,a[i]}' ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_species_rel_abund/raw_${file} | sed 's/ /\t/g' | sed "s/$/\t${NAME}/" > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_species_rel_abund/rel_abund_${file} done

Cat together all of the sample files by taxonomic level. These files can be used for taxonomic analysis in R. cat ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_order_rel_abund/rel_abund_* > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/order_rel_abund.tsvcat ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_family_rel_abund/rel_abund_* > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/family_rel_abund.tsvcat ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_sub_family_rel_abund/rel_abund_* > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/sub_family_rel_abund.tsvcat ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_genus_rel_abund/rel_abund_* > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/genus_rel_abund.tsvcat ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_species_rel_abund/rel_abund_* > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/species_rel_abund.tsv cat ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_order_rel_abund/rel_abund_* > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/order_rel_abund.tsv cat ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_family_rel_abund/rel_abund_* > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/family_rel_abund.tsv cat ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_sub_family_rel_abund/rel_abund_* > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/sub_family_rel_abund.tsv cat ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_genus_rel_abund/rel_abund_* > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/genus_rel_abund.tsv cat ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/trembl_species_rel_abund/rel_abund_* > ./uniprot_contig_virome_trembl_rel_abund/species_rel_abund.tsv

After the environmental background removal, we also wanted to use the program MEGAN to see what percent of our un-assebmled reads were unknown compared to the NCBI non-redundant database (meaning they had no hits to the database). The following outlines our BLAST approach. echo Subsampling clean fasta files for MEGAN analysis...mkdir ./neg_clean_seqs_blastn_for_MEGANmkdir ./neg_clean_subsample_blastn_for_MEGAN echo Subsampling clean fasta files for MEGAN analysis... mkdir ./neg_clean_seqs_blastn_for_MEGAN mkdir ./neg_clean_subsample_blastn_for_MEGAN

These need to be subsampled mainly for speed purposes, but also for normalization. for file in $(ls ./negative_clean_seqs); do seqtk sample ./negative_clean_seqs/$file 2500 > ./neg_clean_subsample_blastn_for_MEGAN/${file}done for file in $(ls ./negative_clean_seqs); do seqtk sample ./negative_clean_seqs/$file 2500 > ./neg_clean_subsample_blastn_for_MEGAN/${file} done

Rename the files. echo Renaming the clean files for MEGAN...for i in $(ls ./neg_clean_subsample_blastn_for_MEGAN); do mv ./neg_clean_subsample_blastn_for_MEGAN/ echo Renaming the clean files for MEGAN... for i in $(ls ./neg_clean_subsample_blastn_for_MEGAN); do mv ./neg_clean_subsample_blastn_for_MEGAN/"${i}" ./neg_clean_subsample_blastn_for_MEGAN/"${i/%.*/.fa}" done echo BlastNing clean subsampled files for MEGAN analysis... run.blast.parallel.clean.for.MEGAN () { blastn -query ./neg_clean_subsample_blastn_for_MEGAN/${1} -out ./neg_clean_seqs_blastn_for_MEGAN/${1} -db ./references/ncbi/nt -outfmt 5 -num_threads 2 -evalue 1e-3 } export -f run.blast.parallel.clean.for.MEGAN ls ./neg_clean_subsample_blastn_for_MEGAN/ | xargs -I {} --max-procs=128 sh -c 'run.blast.parallel.clean.for.MEGAN' wait echo Renaming blastn output for MEGAN... for i in $(ls ./neg_clean_seqs_blastn_for_MEGAN); do mv ./neg_clean_seqs_blastn_for_MEGAN/"${i}" ./neg_clean_seqs_blastn_for_MEGAN/"${i/%.fa/.xml}" done
From here we used the MEGAN GUI to determine the ration of unknown reads. It is important to note that we manually only chose all of the samples except the backgroup control samples.
Data was imported using the 'Import From Blast' option. The unknown read counts were taken from the following categories: Not Assigned, No Hits, Low Complexity.

This is the only time we used MEGAN for virome analysis. In order to calculate unknown reads for the Whole Metagenome samples, we performed a similar analysis, blasting the decontaminated, trimmed reads subsampled at 2500 against the nt database with an e-value of 1e-3 and importing the blast files into MEGAN.
